name: Qays ibn Musahir
title: The Brave Ambassador
shortDescription: The trustworthy messenger of Imam Hussain (AS) to Kufa who tore the letter so it wouldn't fall into enemy hands and defended the Imam from the palace roof.
fullStory:
Qays ibn Musahir al-Saydawi was one of the noble, brave, and loyal figures of Kufa. He played a very key role in transmitting messages between Imam Hussain (AS) and the Shias of Kufa. Qays, along with Abd al-Rahman ibn Abdullah, took the last invitation letters from the Kufans to the Imam in Mecca. When the Imam sent Muslim ibn Aqeel to Kufa, Qays was his guide on this dangerous journey.

After Imam Hussain (AS) departed from Mecca towards Kufa, the Imam wrote a letter to Muslim and the Shias of Kufa at the stopping place of Hajir and entrusted it to Qays. Qays rode towards Kufa with utmost speed and bravery, but was arrested in the Qadisiyya region by Husayn ibn Numayr. Knowing that if the letter fell into enemy hands, the names of the Shias would be exposed, Qays immediately and astutely tore the Imam's letter into pieces or swallowed it.

He was brought bound before Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad. Ibn Ziyad angrily asked: 'Why did you tear the letter?' Qays firmly replied: 'So that you wouldn't know what was written in it'. Ibn Ziyad, frustrated at failing to get the information, said: 'You must go up the pulpit and curse Hussain and his father, otherwise I will cut you to pieces'.

Qays seized this opportunity, went up the pulpit of the Kufa mosque, and after praising God, shouted loudly: 'O people of Kufa! Hussain ibn Ali is the best of God's creation and the son of Fatima, the daughter of the Messenger of God. I am his messenger to you; so help him. May God's curse be upon Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad and his father!'.

Ibn Ziyad, extremely infuriated by these brave and revealing words, ordered Qays to be thrown from the roof of the governor's palace. The pure body of this brave ambassador was shattered, and he attained martyrdom. When the news of his martyrdom reached Imam Hussain (AS) on his journey, the Imam wept profusely and said: 'O God, grant us and our Shias a noble resting place'.
quoteArabic: أَيُّهَا النّاسُ، إِنَّ هذَا الحُسَيْنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ خَيْرُ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ... فَأَجيبوهُ
quote: O people! Hussain ibn Ali is the best of God's creation... so answer his call and help him.
